scheme_name: Simon Sing Horo Special Post-Matric Scholarship
page_title: Simon Sing Horo Special Post-Matric Scholarship

details: The scheme "Simon Sing Horo Special Post-Matric Scholarship" was launched by the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Welfare Department, Government of Assam. The scheme aims to provide a one-time incentive of ₹10,000/- each to the students belonging to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community who have cleared High School Leaving Certificate (HSLC) or Higher Secondary School Leaving Certificate (HSSLC) during the current year, so that they are motivated to continue their further education. The applications for this scheme are accepted online.
benefits: The benefit is a one-time incentive of ₹10,000/- each.
eligibility: The applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam.
The applicant must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community.
The applicant must have passed one of the following examinations -
(a) High School Leaving Certificate (HSLC) examinations from the Board of Secondary Education (SEBA) or an equivalent educational board recognized by the Government of Assam during the current year.

(b) The applicant must have passed the Higher Secondary School Leaving Certificate (HSSLC) examinations in any stream from the Assam Higher Secondary Education Council (AHSEC) or an equivalent educational board recognized by the Government of Assam during the current year.
The applicant's family income must not exceed ₹5,00,000/- per annum.

*The applicant applying for this incentive scheme shall also be eligible for the normal Post-Matric Scholarship, Financial Assistance for Higher Studies, and Financial Assistance for Auxiliary Nurse Midwife (ANM)/General Nursing and Midwifery (GNM)/Technical Courses, if eligible, in the same year.

online: The applicant must first open the official Tea Tribes Welfare Department URL, www.ttwd.assam.gov.in, and then click the "Online Application" link to be redirected to the
New users must register themselves on the online portal, while existing users will log in using the username and password created during prior registration.
Once the applicant is logged into the Dashboard, they must select the appropriate scholarship scheme from the dropdown menu available.
Enter "Personal Details" where all required information in the application form must be accurately provided.
Location data, including District, Assembly Constituency, and Sub-Division, must be selected, along with other essential address details like Village/Town.
After filling in all necessary fields in this step, click on the "Save Form Details" button to proceed further with the application.
Under the "Upload Documents" tab, upload all required supporting documents in the specified image formats (e.g., JPG/JPEG).
All uploaded documents must adhere to specified maximum file sizes (e.g., photo maximum 50 KB, other documents generally 100 KB) before they can be saved.
Enter the Bank Account Number and the name of the bank under the "Update Bank Details" tab.
Details essential for electronic transfer, specifically the Bank Branch Name and the corresponding IFSC Code, must be accurately provided.
Once all bank details have been correctly entered, click on the "Save Bank Details" button to confirm the information.
"Preview Application" displays all details entered in the form for a thorough and final check.
After cross-checking all information and uploaded documents for accuracy, the applicant must confirm the application by clicking the "Proceed with Submit Application" button.
Upon successful submission, an application number will be visible on the screen, allowing the applicant to view the status under the "APPLICATION STATUS" tab.

documents_required: Passport Size Photo (Maximum 50 KB in JPEG/JPG/PNG Format).
Date Of Birth Certificate (Maximum 100 KB in JPEG/JPG/PNG Format).
Original Copy of High School Leaving Certificate (HSLC)/Higher Secondary School Leaving Certificate (HSSLC) Mark-sheet (Maximum 100 KB in JPEG/JPG/PNG Format).
Original Copy of High School Leaving Certificate (HSLC) Pass Certificate (Maximum 100 KB in JPEG/JPG/PNG Format).
Caste Certificate (Maximum 100 KB in JPEG/JPG/PNG Format).
First Page of Bank Passbook (Maximum 100 KB in JPEG/JPG/PNG Format).
Undertaking On Annual Income (As per attached format, Maximum 100 KB in JPEG/JPG/PNG Format) (To be submitted by Parent/Guardian).
Admission/Fee Receipt (Proving Continuation of Studies in Next Level).

faqs.question: If I have successfully cleared the Higher Secondary School Leaving Certificate examination under the Assam Higher Secondary Education Council this year, will I meet the criteria for application?
faqs.answer: The applicant must have passed the Higher Secondary School Leaving Certificate (HSSLC) examinations from the Assam Higher Secondary Education Council (AHSEC) or an equivalent board recognized by the Government of Assam during the current year.

faqs.question: Is there a restriction on the number of my siblings who can also apply and receive this financial incentive if they meet all the necessary qualifications?
faqs.answer: The incentive shall be paid irrespective of the total number of students per family who successfully pass the High School Leaving Certificate (HSLC) or Higher Secondary School Leaving Certificate (HSSLC) examinations in the same year.

faqs.question: Besides applying for this particular incentive scheme, can I simultaneously be considered for the general Post-Matric Scholarship scheme during the current academic year?
faqs.answer: Yes. Candidates applying for this incentive scheme are also eligible for the normal Post-Matric Scholarship, provided they meet the specific eligibility requirements for that scheme.

faqs.question: What is the mandatory income threshold that my family’s annual earnings must adhere to for me to be considered eligible for this benefit?
faqs.answer: The annual family income of the applicant’s parent or guardian must not exceed ₹5,00,000/- (₹5 Lakh) per annum.

faqs.question: If I am currently pursuing an Auxiliary Nurse Midwife (ANM) or General Nursing and Midwifery (GNM) course, am I allowed to apply for that financial assistance scheme as well as this incentive?
faqs.answer: Yes. The candidate remains eligible for financial assistance schemes related to Auxiliary Nurse Midwife (ANM), General Nursing and Midwifery (GNM), or Technical Courses in the same year, if they meet the qualifications.

faqs.question: What specific designation must the official hold who is responsible for certifying or issuing the Caste certificate required for the application process?
faqs.answer: The valid Caste certificate must be duly certified or issued by the District Commissioner of the concerned district, or by the specific officer designated by the District Commissioner.

faqs.question: If I have successfully cleared the High School Leaving Certificate examination under the Board of Secondary Education, Assam this year, does that satisfy the educational criterion for this application?
faqs.answer: The applicant must have passed the High School Leaving Certificate (HSLC) examinations from the Board of Secondary Education, Assam (SEBA), or an equivalent educational board recognized by the Government of Assam during the current year.

faqs.question: Do I need to provide any specific documentation to demonstrate that I have moved forward and enrolled myself in the next academic level of education?
faqs.answer: Yes. The student must provide official admission receipts that serve as proof of the continuation of his or her studies in the next level of education.

faqs.question: Is it necessary for the applicant seeking this financial assistance to be a permanent legal inhabitant of the state of Assam?
faqs.answer: Yes. The candidate applying for the scheme must be a permanent resident of Assam.

faqs.question: Is there a requirement for my parent or guardian to formally declare their income status as part of the overall application materials?
faqs.answer: Yes. An undertaking concerning the annual family income must be submitted by the Parent or Guardian.

faqs.question: If I am interested in applying for financial support for specialized training courses like Financial Assistance for Higher Studies, can I also receive this incentive?
faqs.answer: Yes. The candidates applying for this incentive scheme are also eligible for Financial Assistance for Higher Studies, provided they meet the respective eligibility criteria.

faqs.question: What specific social or tribal designation must I belong to in order to qualify for consideration under this particular scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community.

faqs.question: What kind of proof is required to substantiate my claim of belonging to the appropriate tribal community or group?
faqs.answer: The applicant must produce a valid Caste certificate to substantiate community membership.
